title: Conjecture 1.2 (Georgakopoulos–Papasoglu)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ and all $c\geq 0$, there exists $L,C\geq 0$, such that if a graph $G$ does not contain $H$ as a $c$-fat minor, then $G$ admits an $(L,C)$-quasi-isometry to a graph with no $H$ minor.
context_text: Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu proposed this as a converse to the observation that quasi-isometries preserve minor containment up to fat-ness. The conjecture was verified for $H\in\{K_3, K_{1,m}, K_{2,3}, K_4^-\}$ but subsequently disproved in general by Davies, Hickingbotham, Illingworth, and McCarty, whose counterexample $H$ contains a clique of size 15. The present paper proves a related but distinct 'coarse graph theory analogue' in which the conclusion is quasi-isometry to a graph of bounded line-width rather than to a graph with no $H$ minor.
paper_contribution: Proves a 'coarse graph theory analogue': for every finite tree $H$ and every $c$, there exist $k,L,C$ such that every graph not containing $H$ as a $c$-fat minor admits an $(L,C)$-quasi-isometry to a graph with line-width at most $k$; and proves the matching converse. This replaces 'no $H$ minor' with 'line-width $\leq k$' and is restricted to tree $H$, so it does not resolve Conjecture 1.2 itself.
notes: The paper explicitly labels this item 'False conjecture', since it was disproved in general by arXiv:2405.09383. The attributed year 2023 reflects the arXiv preprint arXiv:2305.07456; the journal publication appeared in Combinatorica 45 (2025).
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. III. Excluding a fat tree

title: Informal question: validity of Conjecture 1.2 for trees and planar graphs
statement_text: Is Conjecture 1.2 (Georgakopoulos–Papasoglu) true when $H$ is a tree, or more generally when $H$ is a planar graph?
context_text: After noting that Conjecture 1.2 was disproved in general with a counterexample $H$ containing a clique of size 15, the authors remark that 'there remains hope that 1.2 is true for some interesting graphs $H$', and parenthetically ask '(Trees? Planar graphs?)'. The paper's own main result handles trees but with a weaker conclusion (bounded line-width rather than no $H$ minor), so the original formulation of Conjecture 1.2 for trees remains open.
notes: Appears as a parenthetical remark in the introduction rather than a formally labelled environment.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. III. Excluding a fat tree
